extends.new-data: Source reports 4MW+ of rooftop solar installed via Solarize Ann Arbor with 540+ residential installations in Year 3.
Source confirms testimony submitted in U-20836 plus three additional MPSC cases (U-21172, U-21297, U-21376) challenging rates and promoting clean energy.
Source reports an Energy Options Analysis commissioned and a DOE grant submitted to formally create the SEU, with decisions expected mid-2024.
Source confirms $2.5M federal funding secured, RFP released, vendors selected (Enerlogics and Melink Solar) for ~3MW on Wheeler, Water/Wastewater, and airport; 1MW at Parks facilities underway.
Source reports DOE planning grant won for Bryant district geothermal loop design and NREL support secured for Veterans Park geothermal data needs study.
Source reports $500K MI-HOPE grant won, 19 households receiving improvements, Bryant Community Center targeted as carbon-negative building, and $580K DOE geothermal planning grant awarded.
Source reports 22 contractors statewide now hold Michigan Saves Electrification Badge and a 30+ contractor convening was held to discuss rebates and best practices.
Source reports $3M in federal aid secured with Ann Arbor Housing Commission to advance net-zero energy affordable housing and geothermal studies at two AAHC sites.
Source reports $54K state grant received for first curbside/utility-pole EV chargers, city light fleet reached 20% electric, and first heavy electric truck introduced.
Source confirms commercial energy and water benchmarking ordinance implemented, described as the first in the State of Michigan.
Source confirms $3M federal aid secured in collaboration with Ann Arbor Housing Commission for net-zero energy affordable housing.
Source reports 3.1 miles of new/improved bike lanes, 2.5 miles of sidewalk gaps filled, Ann Arbor achieved Gold Walk Friendly and Gold Bicycle Friendly Community designations.
Source reports approximately 400 acres rezoned to TC1 (transit corridor) status.
Source reports community passed a millage to support TheRide in improving and expanding public transit, effective 2024.
Source reports three community working sessions held, circular economy GIS map updated, video series launched, and co-design process planned for Year 4.
Source reports A2R3 returnable containers program maintained at four restaurants, EPA grant applied for, and partnership with Perpetual on reusable landscape design.
Source reports second resilience hub completed at Bryant Neighborhood Community Center and EPA grant applied for to formally launch a resilience network and expand hubs county-wide.
Source reports Northside hub was activated as emergency preparedness shelter during February ice storm for five days.
Source reports approximately 5,750 trees planted/distributed to date and a USDA Forest Service Urban Forestry IRA grant proposal submitted for canopy assessment and tree nursery feasibility.
Source reports two additional air quality monitors installed (total now four) and a data visualization platform launched at a2gov.org/airdata.
Source reports Ambassador program grew to 85 members with next cohort targeting 100+, and $229K AmeriCorps grant secured to bring 10 members to OSI.
Source reports over $70,000 granted through the SA2T program in Year 3 for community climate activities.
Source reports $300K in grants provided to local housing providers for energy efficiency improvements for lower-income residents.
Source reports active promotion of Michigan's adoption of the 2021 International Model Building Code.
Source reports AAATA propulsion study supported to identify solutions for carbon-free busing.
Source names full OSI team (13 staff) and reports $229K AmeriCorps grant, CDP A-List recognition for second year, and Michigan Green Communities Gold Level award.
Source reports collaboration with IBEW/NECA on 'The Future is Electric' campaign, informational video series, and workforce development conversations initiated.
Source reports $3M federal aid secured for net-zero affordable housing and geothermal studies at two AAHC sites supported.
Source reports DDA won League of Michigan Bicyclists Outstanding Infrastructure Award for most improved bicycle-friendly infrastructure.
Source reports UM purchased equivalent of 39% of electricity from renewables in 2022 (offsetting 6% of community emissions) and joint RFP released for up to 20MW solar at UM sites.
Source reports Washtenaw County coordinated on GHG inventory completion and $4K waste reduction sponsorship secured for A2R3.
Source reports AAPS GHG inventory completed and small grant program launched to support sustainability projects in public schools.
Source references ReDirect (likely related organization) providing three summer fellows for sustainability behavior change activities.

new-entities.title: A2ZERO Home Energy Advisor Program
new-entities.rationale: New pilot service with Elevate and EcoWorks providing residents personalized home electrification assessments and contractor support.

new-entities.title: Annual Electrification Expo
new-entities.rationale: New annual public event launched in Year 3 with 25 vendors and ~450 attendees to promote home electrification.

new-entities.title: Wheeler Center Landfill Solar Project
new-entities.rationale: Planned 20MW solar installation on City's capped landfill, potentially one of the largest landfill solar projects nationally.

new-entities.title: Commercial Solar Program (2030 District Partnership)
new-entities.rationale: New program launched with the 2030 District to incentivize commercial solar adoption in Washtenaw County.

new-entities.title: Ann Arbor Heating Franchise Initiative
new-entities.rationale: New City-led process to ensure all residents have access to safe, clean, affordable heating and cooling choices.

new-entities.title: Home Energy Rating Disclosure Policy
new-entities.rationale: Drafted policy requiring energy rating disclosure at time of home listing, targeting introduction in summer 2023.

new-entities.title: Aging in Place Efficiently (AIP) Program
new-entities.rationale: Pilot program completing energy efficiency and aging-in-place retrofits for 17 lower-income homeowners, now closed pending refunding.

new-entities.title: Resident Race to Zero Energy
new-entities.rationale: City's first residential energy reduction competition, completed in Year 3 with over 100 participants.

new-entities.title: LED Streetlight Conversion Program
new-entities.rationale: City-wide streetlight LED replacement program funded by $980K SEMCOG grant, starting fall 2023.

new-entities.title: Green Rental Housing Initiative
new-entities.rationale: New program under development to establish energy and sustainability standards for rental housing in Ann Arbor.

new-entities.title: A2ZERO Plant-Based Challenge
new-entities.rationale: Week-long community challenge promoting plant-forward diets with ~200 participants and 300+ event attendees.

new-entities.title: Pollinator-Aware Yard Care Program
new-entities.rationale: New program restructured from No Mow May with 250+ registered participants supporting urban biodiversity.

new-entities.title: Ann Arbor Resilience Network
new-entities.rationale: Emerging network of community organizations exploring coordinated resilience hub expansion across Washtenaw County.

new-entities.title: Community Climate Action Millage
new-entities.rationale: 20-year voter-approved millage passed November 2022, making Ann Arbor the 8th U.S. city to self-tax for local climate action.

new-entities.title: Washington Street Bike Boulevard
new-entities.rationale: New protected bike infrastructure project funded by $200K SEMCOG grant, starting fall 2025.

new-entities.title: A2ZERO Collaborators Network
new-entities.rationale: Growing network of 120+ organizations engaged as planning and implementation partners for A2ZERO strategies.

new-entities.title: Elevate
new-entities.rationale: Nonprofit partner co-piloting the Home Energy Advisor service with OSI and EcoWorks.

new-entities.title: EcoWorks
new-entities.rationale: Nonprofit partner co-piloting the Home Energy Advisor service with OSI and Elevate.

new-entities.title: Live Zero Waste
new-entities.rationale: Nonprofit partner maintaining the A2R3 returnable containers program at local restaurants.

new-entities.title: DOE Grant Application for Sustainable Energy Utility (2023)
new-entities.rationale: Federal DOE grant submitted to formally create the SEU; decisions expected mid-2024.

theme-connections: Community Climate Action Millage passage unlocks new residential rebate and incentive funding that spans Strategies 1, 2, and 3.
Bryant neighborhood is consolidating multiple strategy threads—geothermal, carbon-negative building, weatherization, and resilience hub—into a single replicable decarbonization model.

MPSC utility case interventions (U-20836, U-21172, U-21297, U-21376) link grid decarbonization goals in Strategy 1 to rate equity and distributed generation access across all electrification strategies.
Workforce development through IBEW/NECA and Michigan Saves badging ties contractor capacity building in Strategy 2 to the pace of building efficiency retrofits in Strategy 3.
Resilience hub network expansion and EPA grant application connect Strategy 6 infrastructure goals to county-wide climate collaboration emerging across all strategies.
